footnote: Titles count every German women's championship back to 1974 — the pre-Bundesliga finals era included; East German titles don't count. Never-champions count from their first Frauen-Bundesliga match (the national league began in 1990). The 14 clubs shown are the 2025–26 season's until newcomers Stuttgart and Mainz — both first-time top-flight clubs — kick off in the autumn.

teams.name: Bayer Leverkusen
teams.firstGameNote: Demolished 9–0 at Duisburg in their 2010 debut — and still recovered to stay up; the section descends from nine-time champions SSG Bergisch Gladbach via two moves, but none of that silverware traveled.

teams.name: Bayern Munich

lastTitle.clinch: won 3–2 at Union Berlin's Alte Försterei — Giulia Gwinn's 84th-minute winner — for the earliest title clinch in league history, mid-unbeaten-season

teams.lineage: The 1976 championship predates the Bundesliga — a 4–2 extra-time final win over Tennis Borussia Berlin in Siegen.

teams.name: Carl Zeiss Jena
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 3–2 to Hamburg in FF USV Jena's 2008 Bundesliga debut; the lineage won the last-ever East German championship in 1991 — a title no one can win again — and went back down in 2026.

teams.name: Eintracht Frankfurt

lastTitle.clinch: won 5–1 on the final day — a Birgit Prinz hat-trick — to edge Duisburg by a point for 1. FFC Frankfurt's seventh crown

teams.lineage: 1. FFC Frankfurt (seven titles, 1999–2008) merged into Eintracht Frankfurt on July 1, 2020; the honours moved with the team.

teams.name: SC Freiburg
teams.firstGameNote: Thrashed 0–8 at home by Duisburg in their 1998 debut before fewer than 200 fans; the academy that raised Gwinn and Bühl has since managed third place and two lost cup finals.

teams.name: Hamburger SV
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 6–1 at Duisburg in 1997 after sneaking in as a late replacement entry; the section was withdrawn entirely in 2012 and returned in 2025 — the same weekend the HSV men went up.

teams.name: TSG Hoffenheim
teams.firstGameNote: Beat fellow newcomers Sindelfingen 1–0 in their 2013 debut on a 90th-minute Mana Iwabuchi goal; two third-place finishes are the high-water mark.

teams.name: 1. FC Köln
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 6–2 at Werder Bremen in a debut between two first-timers; the absorbed Brauweiler lineage won the 1997 championship, but that title stays on the predecessor's honours list.

teams.name: 1. FC Nürnberg
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 5–1 to Werder Bremen in their 2023 debut at the Max-Morlock-Stadion; relegated in year one, straight back up, and safe in 2026.

teams.name: RB Leipzig
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 at Köln in their 2023 debut, seven years after the section was founded four divisions down — the women's arm of the Red Bull project is still chasing a first trophy.

teams.name: SGS Essen
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 4–0 at Turbine Potsdam in their 2004 debut as SG Essen-Schönebeck; 22 straight top-flight seasons and Germany's finest academy later, 2026 relegation hit the league's last independent women's club.

teams.name: Union Berlin
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 with Nürnberg before 11,242 at the Alte Försterei in their 2025 debut — promoted with the best crowds in Germany while still a second-division club.

teams.name: Werder Bremen
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Köln 6–2 from two goals down in a 2015 debut between two first-timers; a first cup final arrived in 2025, lost to Bayern before 45,246 in Cologne.

lastTitle.clinch: hammered Carl Zeiss Jena 10–1 on the penultimate matchday, Alexandra Popp leading the rout, to put title number seven out of reach
